title: Content-hash caching + early cutoff
analysis:
The task requires adding content-hash caching with early cutoff to the evaluation engine. The existing codebase has strong foundations: ContentHash (XXH3-128) infrastructure in reify-types/src/hash.rs, Value::content_hash() with proper NaN/-0 normalization in value.rs, Freshness enum for cache state tracking, VersionId for snapshot lineage, and PersistentMap for efficient structural sharing.

Key components to build: (1) NodeId - unified identifier for any graph node (value cell, constraint, realization) since cache needs to key on any node type; (2) CachedResult - enum storing different evaluation result kinds (Value+DeterminacyState, Satisfaction, GeometryHandleId); (3) NodeCache - per-node cache entry with result, result_hash, freshness, dependency_trace, basis_version; (4) CacheStore - HashMap-backed store managing all node caches with get/put/invalidate; (5) EvalOutcome - Changed vs Unchanged signal controlling dirty propagation; (6) Content hashing for Satisfaction and GeometryHandleId results; (7) Integration via Engine::eval_cached() method using cache with version fast path and early cutoff.

DependencyTrace (from task 11) is referenced in NodeCache. Since task 11 is a prerequisite, I'll define a minimal stub in deps.rs if it doesn't exist. The current Engine::eval() evaluates all nodes unconditionally in two passes (params then lets). The cached version needs to: check cache before each node, compare result hashes after evaluation, and return Changed/Unchanged to control propagation. The early cutoff test scenario (e.g., 'let x = if a > 0 then 1 else 1') requires that when 'a' changes but 'x' evaluates to the same result, downstream nodes of 'x' are not re-evaluated.

The integration requires adding a CacheStore field to Engine and a new eval_cached() method that accepts a VersionId. The original eval() remains unchanged for backward compatibility. Tests will use the TopologyTemplateBuilder and CompiledModuleBuilder patterns from reify-test-support.

--- POST-REVIEW AMENDMENT ---
Two blocking issues were identified:
1. try_fast_path_hit_when_same_version test only asserts result.is_some() but never verifies the returned value matches what was stored. A bug returning a wrong CachedResult would pass silently.
2. clear_dependents_dirty(cell) unconditionally removes a node from dirty if it reads `cell`, but doesn't verify that `cell` is the ONLY reason the node is dirty. In a diamond-dependency graph (e.g., y reads both a and x), when x early-cutoffs, y gets incorrectly cleared from dirty even though it also directly reads a (which DID change). Fix: track per-node dirty reasons as a set of ValueCellIds; only clear a node from dirty when ALL its reasons have been resolved by early cutoffs.

prerequisites.description: Create crates/reify-eval/src/deps.rs with minimal DependencyTrace stub (task 11 dependency). DependencyTrace tracks which ValueCellIds a node read during evaluation. Define: pub struct DependencyTrace { pub reads: Vec<ValueCellId> } with Clone, Debug, Default derives. Register as pub mod deps in lib.rs.

Add content_hash() method to Satisfaction enum in crates/reify-types/src/value.rs. Domain-separate the three variants: Satisfied -> [10, 0], Violated -> [10, 1], Indeterminate -> [10, 2]. This follows the existing Value::content_hash() pattern with tag bytes.

Add content_hash() method to GeometryHandleId in crates/reify-types/src/geometry.rs. Hash the u64 id with a domain-separation tag byte [11] followed by the id as le_bytes. This serves as a proxy hash for geometry results (we can't hash OCCT shapes directly).

steps.description: Write failing tests for NodeId enum in cache.rs: construction from ValueCellId, ConstraintNodeId, RealizationNodeId via From impls; PartialEq across variants; Debug/Clone/Hash derives for use as HashMap key. Test that NodeId::Value(vcid) != NodeId::Constraint(cnid) even if underlying strings overlap.

Define NodeId enum in crates/reify-eval/src/cache.rs with three variants: Value(ValueCellId), Constraint(ConstraintNodeId), Realization(RealizationNodeId). Derive Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq, Hash. Implement From<ValueCellId>, From<ConstraintNodeId>, From<RealizationNodeId>. Register pub mod cache in lib.rs.

Write failing tests for CachedResult enum: construction of Value variant with (Value, DeterminacyState), Satisfaction variant, GeometryHandle variant. Test Clone and Debug. Test CachedResult::content_hash() returns correct hash for each variant - Value variant delegates to Value::content_hash() combined with DeterminacyState hash, Satisfaction variant delegates to Satisfaction::content_hash(), GeometryHandle variant uses GeometryHandleId::content_hash().

Define CachedResult enum with three variants: Value(Value, DeterminacyState), Satisfaction(Satisfaction), GeometryHandle(GeometryHandleId). Derive Clone, Debug. Implement content_hash() method that domain-separates each variant (tag bytes [20], [21], [22]) and combines with the inner value's content hash. For DeterminacyState, hash the discriminant as a byte.

Write failing tests for NodeCache struct: construction with all fields (result, result_hash, freshness, dependency_trace, basis_version); verify result_hash matches CachedResult::content_hash() of the result; Clone and Debug work correctly.

Define NodeCache struct with fields: result: CachedResult, result_hash: ContentHash, freshness: Freshness, dependency_trace: DependencyTrace, basis_version: VersionId. Derive Clone, Debug. Add NodeCache::new() constructor that computes result_hash from result.content_hash().
Write failing tests for EvalOutcome enum: Changed and Unchanged variants; pattern matching works correctly. Test Display or Debug format.
Define EvalOutcome enum with two variants: Changed and Unchanged. Derive Clone, Copy, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.

Write failing tests for CacheStore: (1) new() creates empty store; (2) put() stores a NodeCache, get() retrieves it by NodeId; (3) get() returns None for missing NodeId; (4) invalidate() removes a cached entry; (5) invalidate() on missing key is a no-op; (6) put() overwrites existing entry; (7) len() and is_empty() report correct counts.

Define CacheStore struct with caches: HashMap<NodeId, NodeCache>. Implement new(), get(&NodeId) -> Option<&NodeCache>, put(NodeId, NodeCache), invalidate(&NodeId), len(), is_empty(), clear(). Use std::collections::HashMap since cache doesn't need structural sharing.

Write failing tests for version fast path: (1) CacheStore::try_fast_path(node, current_version) returns Some(cached_result.clone()) when cache.basis_version == current_version; (2) returns None when basis_version < current_version; (3) returns None when node is not in cache (miss).

Implement CacheStore::try_fast_path(&self, node: &NodeId, current_version: VersionId) -> Option<CachedResult>. Looks up node in cache, compares basis_version with current_version, returns cloned result if they match, None otherwise.

Write failing tests for early cutoff via CacheStore::record_evaluation(): (1) When new result has same content hash as cached result -> returns EvalOutcome::Unchanged, updates basis_version but keeps old result; (2) When new result has different content hash -> returns EvalOutcome::Changed, updates both result and basis_version; (3) When node has no prior cache (cold start) -> returns EvalOutcome::Changed, creates new cache entry.

Implement CacheStore::record_evaluation(&mut self, node: NodeId, new_result: CachedResult, version: VersionId, trace: DependencyTrace) -> EvalOutcome. Compute new content hash, compare with existing cache entry's hash. If same hash: update basis_version only (early cutoff), return Unchanged. If different or no prior cache: update full cache entry, return Changed.

Write failing test for CacheStore::invalidate_dependents(): given a set of changed NodeIds, invalidate all cached nodes whose DependencyTrace.reads includes any of the changed ValueCellIds. Verify that nodes not depending on changed nodes retain their cache entries.

Implement CacheStore::invalidate_dependents(&mut self, changed: &[ValueCellId]). Iterate all cached nodes; for each, check if dependency_trace.reads intersects with changed set. If so, remove from cache (forcing re-evaluation on next access).

Write failing integration test: cold start cache miss. Build a simple CompiledModule with 2 params and 1 let using TopologyTemplateBuilder. Call Engine::eval_cached() with version 1. Verify all values are computed (no cache hits on first run). Verify CacheStore has entries for all 3 value cells after evaluation.

Add CacheStore field to Engine (initialized empty in Engine::new). Implement Engine::eval_cached(&mut self, module: &CompiledModule, version: VersionId) -> CachedEvalResult that evaluates all nodes, recording each result in the cache via record_evaluation(). CachedEvalResult wraps EvalResult plus a stats struct (cache_hits: usize, cache_misses: usize, early_cutoffs: usize).

Write failing integration test: version fast path. Evaluate a module with eval_cached(version=1). Then call eval_cached(version=1) again (same version). Verify second call has 100% cache hits (all nodes served from cache via version fast path). Verify results are identical.

Integrate version fast path into Engine::eval_cached(). Before evaluating each value cell, call cache.try_fast_path(node_id, version). If hit, use cached result directly (increment cache_hits counter). Otherwise evaluate and record.

Write failing integration test: selective re-evaluation on param change. Build module: param a = 10, param b = 20, let x = a + 1, let y = b + 1. Evaluate with version 1 (populates cache). Change param a to 15 (insert new value), bump version to 2, invalidate dependents of a. Call eval_cached(version=2). Verify x is re-evaluated (new result), y is served from cache (not dependent on a). Verify result correctness.

Add Engine::set_param_and_invalidate(&mut self, param: &ValueCellId, value: Value) that updates the engine's override values and calls cache.invalidate_dependents() for the changed param. Ensure eval_cached respects overridden param values. Wire dependency traces into cache entries during evaluation.

Write failing integration test: early cutoff prevents downstream re-evaluation. Build module: param a (Int) = 5, let x (Int) = if a > 0 then 1 else 1 (always evaluates to 1 regardless of a), let y (Int) = x + 100. Evaluate with version 1 (all computed). Change a from 5 to 10 (still > 0), bump version to 2. Verify x is re-evaluated but result_hash unchanged (early cutoff). Verify y is NOT re-evaluated (served from cache because x didn't change). Check stats: early_cutoffs >= 1.

Complete early cutoff integration in eval_cached(). After re-evaluating a node, call record_evaluation(). If outcome is Unchanged, do NOT invalidate dependents of that node (early cutoff). If outcome is Changed, mark dependents as needing re-evaluation. This requires a two-phase approach: first mark dirty nodes, then evaluate in dependency order, pruning the dirty set when early cutoff occurs.

Fix incomplete assertion in try_fast_path_hit_when_same_version test (reviewer_test_analyst issue). The existing test only asserts result.is_some() but never checks the returned value. Replace the assertion with: if let Some(CachedResult::Value(v, _)) = result { assert_eq!(v, Value::Int(42)); } else { panic!("expected Value variant with Int(42)"); }. This ensures the fast path returns the correct cached result, not merely some value.

Write failing test for diamond-dependency early cutoff correctness bug (reviewer_robustness issue). Build a diamond: param a, let x = if a > 0 then 1 else 1 (reads: [a]), let y = a + x (reads: [a, x]). Evaluate with version 1 (all computed, a=5, x=1, y=6). Change a from 5 to 10, bump to version 2. x early-cutoffs (result still 1). The BUG: clear_dependents_dirty(x_id) removes y from dirty even though y ALSO reads a (which DID change). Assert y is re-evaluated and produces the CORRECT value y = 10 + 1 = 11 (not the stale cached y = 6). This test should FAIL against the current clear_dependents_dirty implementation, proving the bug.

Fix clear_dependents_dirty correctness bug by switching to dirty-reasons tracking. Change CacheStore::dirty from HashSet<NodeId> to HashMap<NodeId, HashSet<ValueCellId>> where the set tracks which changed ValueCellIds caused the node to be dirty. (1) invalidate_dependents: for each node whose reads intersect changed, insert into dirty_reasons[node] the specific changed cells that appear in reads. (2) clear_dependents_dirty(cell): for each dirty node that reads cell, remove cell from that node's reason set; only remove the node from dirty_reasons entirely when its reason set becomes empty. (3) is_dirty: check dirty_reasons.contains_key(node). (4) clear_dirty: remove the node from dirty_reasons entirely. This ensures a node stays dirty as long as ANY of its dirty reasons remain unresolved.

Write additional diamond-dependency test with triple fan-in: param a, param b, let x = if a > 0 then 1 else 1 (reads: [a]), let y = a + b + x (reads: [a, b, x]). Change only a. x early-cutoffs. Verify y is still re-evaluated because y reads a directly (dirty reason a remains). Also verify that if we then change b and x early-cutoffs again, y is re-evaluated because of b. Ensures the dirty-reasons approach handles multiple independent dirty reasons correctly.

Update the invalidate_dependents unit test (step-15, invalidate_dependents_removes_dependent_nodes) to verify dirty_reasons are correctly populated. After invalidating dependents of a, assert that node_x's dirty reasons include a, and node_y (if it read a) includes a. Also verify the existing early_cutoff_prevents_downstream_re_evaluation integration test still passes — y (which only reads x, not a directly) should have its sole dirty reason (x) cleared by early cutoff, confirming existing non-diamond behavior is preserved.

design_decisions.decision: NodeId is an enum in reify-eval, not reify-types
design_decisions.rationale: The individual ID types (ValueCellId, ConstraintNodeId, RealizationNodeId) live in reify-types as they're shared vocabulary. NodeId is a cache-specific unification that's only needed by the eval engine, so it belongs in reify-eval/src/cache.rs to avoid polluting the shared types crate.

The cache is mutable engine-internal state, not snapshot state. It doesn't need structural sharing or O(1) clone. A standard HashMap provides better single-threaded mutation performance and simpler API.

design_decisions.decision: Add eval_cached() as a new method rather than modifying eval()
design_decisions.rationale: Preserves backward compatibility with all existing tests. The original eval() is a simple, correct baseline. eval_cached() adds the incremental optimization. Callers opt in to caching by providing a VersionId.

design_decisions.decision: DependencyTrace is a minimal stub until task 11 fully materializes
design_decisions.rationale: Task 11 is listed as a dependency but deps.rs doesn't exist in this worktree. A minimal struct with reads: Vec<ValueCellId> is sufficient for cache invalidation. The full DependencyTrace from task 11 can replace it later without changing the cache API.

Following the existing pattern in Value::content_hash() where each variant has a unique tag byte prefix. This prevents hash collisions between e.g. a Value result and a Satisfaction result that happen to have similar underlying data.

design_decisions.decision: GeometryHandleId content hash uses the id as proxy, not the actual shape
design_decisions.rationale: OCCT shapes can't be hashed directly (opaque C++ objects). The handle id serves as a stable proxy. If the same operations produce the same handle id pattern, the hash will match. This is noted in the task description as an acceptable approach.

design_decisions.decision: Early cutoff uses two-phase mark-then-evaluate approach
design_decisions.rationale: Phase 1: mark all transitive dependents of changed params as dirty. Phase 2: evaluate dirty nodes in dependency order. After each evaluation, if result hash unchanged (early cutoff), remove that node's dependents from the dirty set. This prevents unnecessary cascading while ensuring correctness.

Stats are essential for testing that caching actually works (e.g., asserting cache_hits == N after second evaluation). They also provide observability for debugging and performance monitoring in production.

design_decisions.decision: Conditional expression 'if a > 0 then 1 else 1' for early cutoff test
design_decisions.rationale: This is the canonical test case from the task description. Both branches produce the same value, so changing 'a' triggers re-evaluation of the let but the result hash is unchanged, demonstrating early cutoff preventing propagation to downstream nodes.

design_decisions.decision: Dirty reasons tracked as HashMap<NodeId, HashSet<ValueCellId>> instead of HashSet<NodeId>
design_decisions.rationale: The original HashSet<NodeId> dirty set loses information about WHY a node is dirty. In a diamond-dependency graph where y reads both a and x, an early cutoff on x would incorrectly clear y from dirty even though a (which DID change) is also a reason y is dirty. Tracking per-node dirty reasons as a set of ValueCellIds ensures a node stays dirty until ALL of its dirty reasons have been resolved by early cutoffs. This is the minimal correct fix — the alternative (re-checking all reads at evaluation time) would be less efficient for large graphs.

reuse.how: Use ContentHash::of(), of_str(), combine() for all result hashing. The existing 128-bit XXH3 implementation provides speed and collision resistance.
Already implements deterministic hashing with NaN canonicalization and -0/+0 normalization for all Value variants. CachedResult::Value delegates directly to this.
NodeCache uses Freshness to track evaluation lifecycle (Final, Intermediate, Pending, Failed). Already defined with Clone, Debug, PartialEq, Eq.
NodeCache::basis_version uses VersionId for version fast path. Already has Copy, Ord, Hash derives.
Build test fixtures for integration tests. The builder pattern allows constructing modules with specific param/let/constraint topologies for testing cache behavior.
Engine::new() requires a ConstraintChecker. MockConstraintChecker provides predetermined results for integration tests.
Convenient test value construction. Use mm() for dimensioned scalars, vcid() for ValueCellId creation in tests.
The pure expression evaluator is called within eval_cached() for each node that needs (re-)evaluation. It computes the Value from a CompiledExpr and ValueMap.
Used in the early cutoff test: 'if a > 0 then 1 else 1' is a Conditional expression where both branches are literals. Already fully implemented in eval_expr.
CachedResult::Value stores (Value, DeterminacyState) pairs, matching the Snapshot::values map pattern.
